decision_summary: The chip temperature (89.26°C) exceeds the 85.0°C limit. Since previous iterations showed diminishing returns, a coordinated multi-variable change is required. I will simultaneously increase the heat spreader's conductivity and its vertical height to enhance both lateral spreading and the thermal cross-section for heat extraction towards the boundaries.

changes.reason: Maximizing spreader conductivity within bounds improves lateral heat distribution, reducing local hotspots under the chip.
Increasing spreader height by 40% expands the thermal conduction path area, lowering overall thermal resistance from the chip to the cooled sides.

expected_effects: Reduction of chip peak temperature below 85.0°C
More uniform temperature distribution across the heat spreader
Improved heat flux towards the left and right Dirichlet boundaries
risk_notes: Approaching upper bound for spreader conductivity may limit future material-based improvements
Increased spreader height reduces clearance to the top boundary; ensure no overlap violations occur in future vertical moves
Diminishing returns may still occur if the bottleneck shifts to the base plate conductivity
